key_metrics: Time to Value (minutes from install to working)

benchmarks.time_to_value_minutes: < 5 for CLI tools, < 15 for SDKs, < 30 for full platforms
benchmarks.documentation_coverage: > 90% of public APIs documented with examples
benchmarks.issue_response_time_hours: < 48 hours for issues from active maintainers
benchmarks.github_stars_growth: 100+ stars in first month = strong signal
benchmarks.paid_conversion_oss: 1–5% of OSS users convert to paid is healthy

best_practices: README must have a working code example in the first 20 lines
Zero-config default — the tool should work before the user reads any documentation
Semantic versioning strictly enforced — breaking changes only in major versions
Changelog maintained per version (CHANGELOG.md or GitHub releases)
TypeScript types shipped with the package — no separate @types/ package for new tools
CLI tools should print --help with examples by default
Local dev setup must work in < 5 minutes with a single command
Error messages must include the fix, not just the problem
Never break backward compatibility in a minor release
common_risks: Dependency hell — transitive dependency conflicts block adoption
Security vulnerabilities in dependencies — npm audit failures block enterprise adoption
Abandoned maintenance — a single unmaintained package blocks upgrade paths
Breaking changes without migration guides cause immediate churn
Documentation rot — examples that no longer work erode trust
Overly complex configuration preventing quick adoption
Not working on Windows (many dev tools are developed on Mac/Linux only)
growth_patterns: Open source first — build community before monetizing
GitHub stars as social proof — encourage starring in README and docs
Hacker News Show HN post — best channel for initial developer awareness
Integration with popular tools (VS Code extension, GitHub Action, Vercel integration)
Technical blog posts showing real use cases on dev.to, Hashnode, personal blogs
Developer conference talks and demo videos
CLI install one-liner (npx, brew, curl | sh) for frictionless adoption

documentation_standards.example_quality: Every example must be copy-pasteable and produce working output
documentation_standards.versioning: Docs must be versioned or at minimum indicate which version they cover

ecosystem_integration_targets: VS Code — editor extension or .vscode/settings.json support
npm — published package with < 50ms require time
recommended_integrations: GitHub — stars, issues, pull requests for community management
Algolia DocSearch — free search for open source documentation sites
Sentry — error tracking in CLI tools and SDKs
Resend — developer notification emails (usage alerts, upgrade prompts)
Stripe — commercial licensing and usage-based billing
